chapter: Chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word

questions.question: Who is Encyclopedia Brown and why is he famous?
questions.answer: Encyclopedia Brown is a young boy known for solving difficult mysteries using his intelligence.

questions.question: Why did people call Leroy Brown 'Encyclopedia'?
questions.answer: Because he knew many facts and never forgot anything he read.

questions.question: What role did Chief Brown play in the story?
questions.answer: Chief Brown was the Chief of Police and Encyclopedia’s father who discussed difficult cases with him.

questions.question: Why did Chief Brown come home worried one evening?
questions.answer: Because he could not solve a mystery related to Tim Nolan.

questions.question: Who was Tim Nolan and why was he suspicious?
questions.answer: Tim Nolan was suspected of being involved in a jewellery robbery years earlier.

questions.question: What kind of business did Nolan run after leaving prison?
questions.answer: He ran a small palm-tree nursery.

questions.question: Who was Daniel Davenport?
questions.answer: Daniel Davenport was Nolan’s friend who was also suspected in the robbery.

questions.question: What important document did Nolan leave before he died?
questions.answer: He left his will on the kitchen table.

questions.question: What strange clue was attached to Nolan’s will?
questions.answer: A calendar sheet containing four unusual words.

questions.question: What were the four coded words written by Nolan?
questions.answer: Nom, Utes, Sweden, and Hurts.

questions.question: Why were the four words confusing to the Browns at first?
questions.answer: Because they appeared to have unrelated meanings.

questions.question: How did Mrs. Brown interpret the words?
questions.answer: She explained them as separate meanings like grammar terms and place names.

questions.question: What made Encyclopedia suspect the words were a code?
questions.answer: He realized they were written on a calendar sheet.

questions.question: What question did Encyclopedia ask his father?
questions.answer: He asked if there was a young fir tree in Nolan’s nursery.

questions.question: Why was the fir tree important in solving the mystery?
questions.answer: Because it represented the fifth word in the coded message.

questions.question: What did the four words actually represent?
questions.answer: They represented the days Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

questions.question: What was the hidden fifth word in the code?
questions.answer: Friday.

questions.question: How did the coded words form the days of the week?
questions.answer: They were formed from the remaining letters after removing the word 'day'.

questions.question: Where was the stolen jewellery finally found?
questions.answer: Under the young fir tree in Nolan’s nursery.

questions.question: Why had Nolan written the coded message?
questions.answer: To secretly tell Davenport where the jewellery was hidden.

questions.question: What skill helped Encyclopedia solve the case quickly?
questions.answer: His sharp observation and logical thinking.

questions.question: Why does Chief Brown trust Encyclopedia’s thinking?
questions.answer: Because Encyclopedia has solved many difficult mysteries before.

questions.question: What does the story reveal about Encyclopedia’s personality?
questions.answer: It shows that he is intelligent, observant, and thoughtful.

questions.question: What lesson can readers learn from this mystery?
questions.answer: Careful observation and reasoning can help solve problems.

questions.question: Why is the story titled 'The Case of the Fifth Word'?
questions.answer: Because solving the mystery depended on identifying the missing fifth word.
